summary: Completed comprehensive frontend testing of CarryOn™ UI redesign. All internal pages (Dashboard, Vault, Messages, Guardian, Settings, Beneficiaries, Checklist, Trustee Services) are working correctly with the new dark theme design. Sidebar navigation with 'MY LEGACY' and 'ACCOUNT' sections is functional. Gold accent highlighting on active navigation items works correctly. Theme toggle (dark/light mode) and logout functionality verified.
passed_tests: Login page loads with dark theme design
OTP verification flow works correctly
Dashboard page renders with new design (sidebar, readiness gauge, quick actions, checklist, activity timeline)
Sidebar navigation displays 'MY LEGACY' and 'ACCOUNT' sections
Active navigation item highlighted with gold color (#E0AD2B)
Dashboard shows Estate Readiness gauge with score
Quick Actions grid displays correctly (Upload Document, Create Message, Add Beneficiary, Ask Guardian)
Immediate Action Checklist preview with progress bar
Activity Timeline displays recent activities
Vault page displays documents in grid layout with filter tabs
Estate Guardian page shows AI chat interface with suggested questions
Settings page shows Profile, Appearance, Notifications, Security sections
Theme toggle works (dark/light mode switching)
Logout functionality redirects to login page
Messages page displays milestone messages in grid
Beneficiaries page shows beneficiary cards
Checklist page shows categorized tasks with progress
Trustee Services page shows pricing plans and additional services
Glass-morphic card design applied consistently
User info displayed in sidebar footer (avatar, name, email)
seed_data_creation: Used existing test user: pete@mitchell.com / password123
context_for_next_testing_agent: UI redesign is complete and working. All internal pages match the prototype design with dark theme (#08090F), gold accents (#E0AD2B), sidebar navigation with MY LEGACY/ACCOUNT sections, and glass-morphic cards. Login flow requires OTP verification - get OTP from backend logs: tail -1 /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log | grep -oP 'OTP for.*: \K\d+'
